title: Capacity consolidation policy and prioritization for dedicated enterprise requests
preview: Problem: Enterprise customers submit dedicated capacity requests that are highly variable — many small, overlapping requests and some burst-only asks — leading to fragmented reservations, inefficient GPU allocations, and missed SLA targets. This ticket defines a consolidation policy and prioritization guidance to re...
text:
Problem: Enterprise customers submit dedicated capacity requests that are highly variable — many small, overlapping requests and some burst-only asks — leading to fragmented reservations, inefficient GPU allocations, and missed SLA targets. This ticket defines a consolidation policy and prioritization guidance to reduce fragmentation, improve utilization, and align reserved throughput and GPU class decisions with clear enterprise SLAs and scaling expectations.

Scope: intake time prioritization rubric, request bundling rules, GPU fitment recommendations (A/B/C bands), temporal smoothing (monthly windowing and predictable burst windows), and fallbacks when capacity or GPU class is constrained. This is product-facing guidance for intake gating and decisioning; implementation will require downstream ENG follow-up for automation and console UI changes.

title: Cycle Pulse & Echo: Capacity Canvas and Slate Shepherding
preview: Problem: In high-velocity cycles we repeatedly hit two friction points: (1) teams over-commit early and then scramble to de-scope mid-cycle, and (2) platform ops can't reliably forecast burst capacity needs for Dedicated on short notice. Scope: establish a repeatable lightweight process and artifacts (Capacity Canva...
text: Problem: In high-velocity cycles we repeatedly hit two friction points: (1) teams over-commit early and then scramble to de-scope mid-cycle, and (2) platform ops can't reliably forecast burst capacity needs for Dedicated on short notice. Scope: establish a repeatable lightweight process and artifacts (Capacity Canvas + Slate Shepherd) that combine quantitative capacity budgeting with qualitative tradeoff surfaces so PMs and Eng leads can make deterministic cycle choices. Goals: reduce mid-cycle scope churn by 30%, shorten decision turnaround for staffing reallocations to <48h, and provide a 2-week predictive window for Dedicated capacity requests. Approach: introduce "Pulse" checkpoints (pre-cycle, week2 pulse, mid-cycle pulse) with clear signals and an "Echo" reserve policy (micro-credits pool + shadow roster). Deliverables: (1) Capacity Canvas template (workload-weighted token-hours...

title: Developer Onboarding Funnel & SDK Playbook — sample apps, guided flows, and measurement
preview:
Summary:
This initiative creates a repeatable onboarding funnel and operational playbook to improve first-time SDK activation, reduce time-to-first-success, and establish measurement for SDK + docs + sample apps. Outcome: a documented playbook, 3 production-ready sample apps (Python, JS, Go), a guided 'first-call' i...
text:
Summary:
This initiative creates a repeatable onboarding funnel and operational playbook to improve first-time SDK activation, reduce time-to-first-success, and establish measurement for SDK + docs + sample apps. Outcome: a documented playbook, 3 production-ready sample apps (Python, JS, Go), a guided 'first-call' interactive quickstart in Console, and an analytics schema to track funnel conversion and retention.

Goals / OKRs: 
- OKR 1: Increase SDK activation rate (trial accounts completing first successful API call with SDK) from 22% -> 45% in Q2.
- OKR 2: Reduce median time-to-first-success from 22 minutes -> <10 minutes.
- OKR 3: Create an evaluation signal for SDK regressions: deploy a weekly smoke test suite covering top 10 endpoints.

Scope & Deliverables (top-level):
1) SDK onboarding playbook (PM + Eng + Design): step-by-step flows for sign-up -> token -> local quickstart ->...

title: Dynamic batch-slot experiment and QSA gap analysis for per-vector pricing
preview: Goal: validate a dynamic batch-slot pricing model that reduces per-vector unit cost for high-throughput customers while preserving latency SLOs and rerank quality. This ticket covers the product spec, experiment design, QSA pre-check for enterprise contract language, and the rollout gating criteria. We want to captu...
text: Goal: validate a dynamic batch-slot pricing model that reduces per-vector unit cost for high-throughput customers while preserving latency SLOs and rerank quality. This ticket covers the product spec, experiment design, QSA pre-check for enterprise contract language, and the rollout gating criteria. We want to capture trade-offs (latency vs unit cost), required runtime changes, and the contractual ask for batch-discount optics in QSA/QSA addenda.

title: Launch Enablement & Release-Notes Matrix for Per-Route Dashboards, Rollouts, and Cost Attribution
preview: Playbook to coordinate the launch of per-route dashboards, rollout controls, and cost breakdowns in the Console. This ticket captures the release-notes matrix, channel scripts for GTM, enablement sprints, training materials, and the cross-team orchestration checklist (Eng, Design, Support, Docs, Sales). Goal: smooth...
text: Playbook to coordinate the launch of per-route dashboards, rollout controls, and cost breakdowns in the Console. This ticket captures the release-notes matrix, channel scripts for GTM, enablement sprints, training materials, and the cross-team orchestration checklist (Eng, Design, Support, Docs, Sales). Goal: smooth GA with clear troubleshooting guides and zero-surprise bill visibility for customers.

title: Enterprise SDK adapter matrix: streaming ergonomics & auth convergence
preview:
Summary:
Consolidate incoming enterprise requests for language-specific SDK adapters and streaming authentication ergonomics into a prioritized, pragmatic roadmap and a repeatable triage/playbook. Customers are asking for better first‑class experience across: Java/.NET/Go/Rust bindings, resumable streaming with toke...
text:
Summary:
Consolidate incoming enterprise requests for language-specific SDK adapters and streaming authentication ergonomics into a prioritized, pragmatic roadmap and a repeatable triage/playbook. Customers are asking for better first‑class experience across: Java/.NET/Go/Rust bindings, resumable streaming with token rotation, client-side reconnect semantics, and enterprise auth patterns (SAML/OIDC token exchange, mTLS, client cert rotation).

Goals:
- Produce a prioritization rubric that balances enterprise security needs, integration complexity, and business impact.
- Define an adapter matrix (language x feature surface) that yields a minimal viable set of SDKs + streaming primitives for high-risk customers.
- Provide engineering-friendly acceptance criteria (API contract, test harness, telemetry) so engineering can scope and plan.
- Surface security and rollout constraints early (K...

title: Capacity reservoir + tenant isolation controls for Dedicated clusters (PRD)
preview: This PRD defines the capacity reservoir concept and tenant isolation controls for Redwood Dedicated clusters. The goal is to provide predictable reserved throughput for paying customers while enabling bounded overflow (bursts) and strong isolation guarantees to prevent noisy-neighbor and overload scenarios. The desi...
text: This PRD defines the capacity reservoir concept and tenant isolation controls for Redwood Dedicated clusters. The goal is to provide predictable reserved throughput for paying customers while enabling bounded overflow (bursts) and strong isolation guarantees to prevent noisy-neighbor and overload scenarios. The design balances utilization efficiency with SLA fidelity and operational simplicity.

title: Initiative Cartography: Prioritization Grid & Scoring for Roadmap Allocation
preview: Create an explicit, multidimensional prioritization framework — called Initiative Cartography — to help PMs and GTM align roadmap bets across latency sensitivity, per-token cost impact, user value, and deployment risk. This ticket captures the scoring design, measurement plan, visualization grid, and an initial audi...
text: Create an explicit, multidimensional prioritization framework — called Initiative Cartography — to help PMs and GTM align roadmap bets across latency sensitivity, per-token cost impact, user value, and deployment risk. This ticket captures the scoring design, measurement plan, visualization grid, and an initial audit of 12 candidate initiatives to validate the model before broader rollout.

title: Model Terrain Pulse — procurement trigger experiments & cohort signal playbook
preview:
Goal: produce an operational playbook and prototype dashboards that translate continuous market signals (model capability, latency & pricing shifts, regional availability) into deterministic procurement triggers and prioritized follow-up work.

Why now: sales + customer success reported three recent prospect losses ...
text:
Goal: produce an operational playbook and prototype dashboards that translate continuous market signals (model capability, latency & pricing shifts, regional availability) into deterministic procurement triggers and prioritized follow-up work.

Why now: sales + customer success reported three recent prospect losses due to sudden provider availability and pricing swings. We need a repeatable, low-friction system to detect when a supplier/model requires procurement action (e.g., reserved capacity, switch, or contract negotiation).

Scope / Deliverables:
- Signal taxonomy & scoring rubric: define 8 signals (availability, price delta, latency delta, quantization support, license change, region outage, API contract mismatch, empirical quality regression) and an algorithmic score that maps to actions (monitor, engage partner, escalate to sales).
- Cohort mapping: categorize customers/worklo...

title: Field Alpha Probe Program: model liveness & sourcing cost tracker
preview:
Objective: run a lightweight, repeatable "alpha probe" program to validate model liveness, true-to-production sourcing cost, and procurement friction signals for 6 target open and partner models across regulated verticals (finserv, healthcare, legal).

Why: existing market scans surface candidate models but do not c...
text:
Objective: run a lightweight, repeatable "alpha probe" program to validate model liveness, true-to-production sourcing cost, and procurement friction signals for 6 target open and partner models across regulated verticals (finserv, healthcare, legal).

Why: existing market scans surface candidate models but do not capture operational liveness (how often binaries/weights are pulled/updated or deprecated), procurement latency (time to license/confirm usage terms), nor real per-customer cost to serve under production-like token distributions. These signals materially affect fallback strategy and Dedicated capacity commitments.

Scope and deliverables: compact playbook and dataset: 1) A runbook for a 6-week alpha probe executed by PM + Solutions Eng + Procurement; 2) A reproducible telemetry harness that measures: liveness (availability checks, patch cadence), provisioning latency (time-t...

title: Quarter orchestration: hosted API + model-fleet sprint conductor
preview: Coordinate quarterly roadmap commitments for Hosted API and the Model Catalog to align OKRs, reduce cross-team dependency risk, and create a lightweight scheduling conductor that automates canary windows and rollout lanes. The goal is a single runnable sprint plan that maps feature deliverables, model onboarding mil...
text: Coordinate quarterly roadmap commitments for Hosted API and the Model Catalog to align OKRs, reduce cross-team dependency risk, and create a lightweight scheduling conductor that automates canary windows and rollout lanes. The goal is a single runnable sprint plan that maps feature deliverables, model onboarding milestones, canary schedules, and capacity hedges so engineering and infra teams can lock resource commits earlier.

title: Onboarding playbook for experimental open models with compatibility matrix
preview: Clarify acceptance criteria and execution steps for onboarding new open-source model variants into the Redwood model catalog. This ticket defines scope, required compatibility metadata, UI and API surface decisions, and acceptance tests for experimental models with nonstandard kernels or quantization. The goal is a ...
text: Clarify acceptance criteria and execution steps for onboarding new open-source model variants into the Redwood model catalog. This ticket defines scope, required compatibility metadata, UI and API surface decisions, and acceptance tests for experimental models with nonstandard kernels or quantization. The goal is a repeatable playbook that reduces release friction and avoids runtime regressions when we expose community models to hosted and dedicated customers.

title: Volume-tier onboarding and special pricing request policy
preview: Define and operationalize an end-to-end policy for onboarding new volume tiers into the public pricebook and handling Special Pricing Requests (SPRs) from Sales. This ticket covers approval gates, required artifacts (SPR form, deal economics worksheet), pricebook versioning, audit trail requirements, and integration...
text: Define and operationalize an end-to-end policy for onboarding new volume tiers into the public pricebook and handling Special Pricing Requests (SPRs) from Sales. This ticket covers approval gates, required artifacts (SPR form, deal economics worksheet), pricebook versioning, audit trail requirements, and integration points with Security Questionnaire reviews when non-standard deployment or data controls are requested.

title: Retention & Revalidation Policy for Redwood-Verified Performance Profiles
preview: As Redwood’s catalog of verified performance profiles grows, we need a clear lifecycle policy that balances signal freshness, operational cost, and compatibility with model pinning. This ticket defines retention windows, automated revalidation cadence, archival behavior, and the UX/notification flow for profile stal...
text: As Redwood’s catalog of verified performance profiles grows, we need a clear lifecycle policy that balances signal freshness, operational cost, and compatibility with model pinning. This ticket defines retention windows, automated revalidation cadence, archival behavior, and the UX/notification flow for profile staleness and republishing. The goal is to ensure customers relying on Redwood-verified latency/throughput/cost numbers have up-to-date guidance while limiting unnecessary rebenching costs.

title: Trajectory & Autoscale Experiments Cadence — Quarterly Roadmap and Runbook
preview: This ticket defines the Q2 cadence and prioritized slate of experiments for improving Dedicated capacity economics via adaptive autoscale and predictive trajectory windows. Goal is to produce a 10-week experimentation plan plus an operations runbook that can be handed to Capacity Ops and SRE for execution and to lin...
text: This ticket defines the Q2 cadence and prioritized slate of experiments for improving Dedicated capacity economics via adaptive autoscale and predictive trajectory windows. Goal is to produce a 10-week experimentation plan plus an operations runbook that can be handed to Capacity Ops and SRE for execution and to link clear OKRs and success metrics back to billing and SLOs. This initiative should align with Console signals (cost per token, prewarm hit-rate) and Runtime telemetry (cold-start latency, queue depth).

title: Release-train comms, ops runbook, and release-notes digest for API/Console/Runtime
preview: Summary: Coordinate a single, consolidated release-train communications and operational runbook that covers milestones, scope-lock signals, cut criteria, release-notes generation, and GTM tasks across API, Console, and Runtime teams. Goal is to reduce ambiguity about last-minute scope decisions, automate a digest fo...
text:
Summary: Coordinate a single, consolidated release-train communications and operational runbook that covers milestones, scope-lock signals, cut criteria, release-notes generation, and GTM tasks across API, Console, and Runtime teams. Goal is to reduce ambiguity about last-minute scope decisions, automate a digest for release notes, and codify a lightweight hypercare plan for the first 72 hours post-rollout.

Background: Recent rollouts had fragmented comms between runtime and console teams resulting in mismatched docs, late bugfix rollouts, and duplicated rollback efforts. This initiative centralizes cutline decisions and creates an automated release-notes digest that compiles: PRs merged after freeze, critical known issues, upgrade notes, and per-team owner blurbs.

Scope:
- Milestone calendar and signoff checklist for MAJOR, MINOR, and PATCH release lanes.
- Cut criteria matrix (fun...

title: Acceptance criteria for structured response protocols and fallback modes
preview: Purpose: Define deterministic, testable acceptance criteria for structured output (JSON/typed responses) and tool/function-calling behavior across three operational modes: synchronous full-response, streaming partial-response, and graceful fallback/error envelopes. This ticket captures requirements clarifications, s...
text: Purpose: Define deterministic, testable acceptance criteria for structured output (JSON/typed responses) and tool/function-calling behavior across three operational modes: synchronous full-response, streaming partial-response, and graceful fallback/error envelopes. This ticket captures requirements clarifications, scope decisions, and explicit testable outcomes the product and engineering teams must satisfy before we mark Structured Output v2 as shippable. The doc focuses on schema negotiation, validation tolerances, error/fallback semantics, streaming checkpoints, and telemetry needed for rollouts.

title: LandingPad sprint allocator and release-train governance
preview: Problem statement: As multiple feature teams target overlapping launch windows across runtime, console, and API surfaces, we lack a single, enforceable runway allocator that ties launch dates to capacity reservations, OKR deadlines, and rollback-safe guardrails. This creates last-minute contention for Dedicated capa...
text:
Problem statement: As multiple feature teams target overlapping launch windows across runtime, console, and API surfaces, we lack a single, enforceable runway allocator that ties launch dates to capacity reservations, OKR deadlines, and rollback-safe guardrails. This creates last-minute contention for Dedicated capacity, elevated latency risk, and misaligned communications for enterprise customers.

Goal: Build the "LandingPad" sprint allocator — a lightweight governance pattern and supporting coordination checklist that: (1) maps launches to capacity lanes (baseline, hedge, emergency), (2) enforces gating criteria per surface (runtime/console/api), (3) publishes a one-page cross-team runway with rollout owners and rollback triggers, and (4) integrates with quarterly OKRs and the release calendar so product planning and platform capacity are aligned.

Scope and deliverables:
- Define ...

title: Procurement Trust Sandbox: SOC2-Minimalist Package + Insurance Offset Pricing Pilot
preview: Run a 6-week pilot that packages a lightweight, machine-readable SOC2 evidence bundle and an insurance-offset credit product for low-to-medium risk vendors. The goal is to validate whether (a) offering a 'Trust Sandbox' — a self-serve minimal SOC2 artifact package + optional short-term insurance offset — reduces pro...
text: Run a 6-week pilot that packages a lightweight, machine-readable SOC2 evidence bundle and an insurance-offset credit product for low-to-medium risk vendors. The goal is to validate whether (a) offering a 'Trust Sandbox' — a self-serve minimal SOC2 artifact package + optional short-term insurance offset — reduces procurement lead time and increases conversion for mid-market customers, and (b) which pricing shape maximizes revenue while keeping legal and risk exposure bounded.

title: Calibration run priority queue, artifact staging, and hot-swap workflow for quant profiles
preview: Problem: As calibration runs (FP8/INT8 sweeps) scale across tenants and model variants, we need a robust orchestration layer in the Console to prioritize runs, stage resulting quant-profiles as promoted or ephemeral artifacts, and support hot-swap promotion without service disruption. Current behavior queues runs FI...
text:
Problem: As calibration runs (FP8/INT8 sweeps) scale across tenants and model variants, we need a robust orchestration layer in the Console to prioritize runs, stage resulting quant-profiles as promoted or ephemeral artifacts, and support hot-swap promotion without service disruption. Current behavior queues runs FIFO and writes artifacts directly to long-term storage which causes delays for critical runs and complicates rollbacks and permissioning.

Goal: Implement a priority-aware queue with tenant and SLA awareness, an intermediate staging area for candidate quant profiles (ephemeral with TTL), and a hot-swap promotion path that updates serving routing without requiring tenant downtime. The feature must include RBAC controls for who can promote artifacts, audit logging for promotions, and retention/GC policies for staged artifacts.

title: Policy Canvas: region priority & tier-fallback visual configurator
preview: Create a Console-first visual editor ("Policy Canvas") that lets customers author routing policies combining region selection, customer tier routing, and fallback model variants. The editor should: 1) surface region priority lists with weighted preference and automatic compatibility checks, 2) allow tier mapping (e....
text: Create a Console-first visual editor ("Policy Canvas") that lets customers author routing policies combining region selection, customer tier routing, and fallback model variants. The editor should: 1) surface region priority lists with weighted preference and automatic compatibility checks, 2) allow tier mapping (e.g., free/prod/enterprise) to model variants and region tiers, 3) configure fallback variants (quantized/cheaper models or lower-tier regions) with stacked priority, 4) run live validation and a preview simulation that shows example request routing across a sample traffic profile, and 5) generate a machine-readable policy JSON for the runtime while preserving a human-friendly visual layout.

title: Post-pilot readout + next-steps plan — Vertical ABM (Regulated Workloads: FinServ + Healthcare)
preview:
Goal
Create a durable post-pilot readout covering (1) pipeline outcomes, (2) lessons learned / what changed in messaging + process, (3) what assets converted, and (4) a proposal to scale ABM to additional regulated segments without pulling in major product scope.

text:
Goal
Create a durable post-pilot readout covering (1) pipeline outcomes, (2) lessons learned / what changed in messaging + process, (3) what assets converted, and (4) a proposal to scale ABM to additional regulated segments without pulling in major product scope.

Audience
Exec staff (Marketing + Sales + Product), Security/GRC, SE leadership. This is intended to become the “v1 playbook” artifact for repeatable regulated outbound.

Pilot snapshot
- Timebox: ~8 weeks (prep + execution + close) May–June 2025
- Verticals: Financial services (primary) + Healthcare/PHI-adjacent (secondary)
- Primary products: Redwood Dedicated + Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem)
- Core buyer set: CISO/Security, Platform/ML Eng, Procurement

Success criteria (from brief)
- Validate repeatable positioning + outbound motion for compliance-driven buyers
- Produce a baseline set of vertical assets (landing page, one...

title: Vertical ABM pilot plan + KPIs (regulated workloads)
preview:
Goal
Run a 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot focused on regulated workloads (initial verticals: Financial Services + Healthcare) to validate repeatable positioning/outbound motion for security/compliance-driven buyers and generate qualified pipeline for Redwood Dedicated + Redwood Private.

In scope

text:
Goal
Run a 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot focused on regulated workloads (initial verticals: Financial Services + Healthcare) to validate repeatable positioning/outbound motion for security/compliance-driven buyers and generate qualified pipeline for Redwood Dedicated + Redwood Private.

In scope

- Measurement: coverage, engagement, meetings, SQLs, POC starts, stage velocity, closed-won signals

Out of scope
- Major new product features (OK: minor docs updates/clarifications + enablement)

tags: Vertical ABM pilot: regulated workloads (FinServ + Healthcare)

preview:
Goal
Launch a customer-facing landing page tailored to security/compliance-driven buyers (finserv + healthcare/PHI-adjacent) as part of the 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot. Page should route to the correct next step (security review call / architecture consult) and link to Sales-ready security materials without making o...
text:

Launch a customer-facing landing page tailored to security/compliance-driven buyers (finserv + healthcare/PHI-adjacent) as part of the 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot. Page should route to the correct next step (security review call / architecture consult) and link to Sales-ready security materials without making over-broad compliance claims.

Primary CTA
“Request a security + architecture review” (form) with secondary CTA: “See security FAQ” (public doc) and gated CTA: “Request evidence pack” (sales-assisted / NDA).

Scope / Requirements
1) Page structure (target: single page, lightweight, skimmable)
- Hero: regulated positioning (performance + compliance posture) with 1 sentence on deployment options.
- Sections:
 - “Deploy the way regulated teams need”: Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private (VPC/on-prem) with clear guidance on when to choose each.
 - “Security & compliance controls”: enc...

title: pricing-boundaries-sso-rbac-contract-clauses-experiment
preview: Design and run a pricing + contract experiment that clarifies when SSO/SAML and RBAC are treated as included features vs paid add-ons, and produce a contract language pack and security questionnaire guidance for sales/legal. Outcome: a repeatable packaging decision framework and a short-run pricing experiment to val...
text: Design and run a pricing + contract experiment that clarifies when SSO/SAML and RBAC are treated as included features vs paid add-ons, and produce a contract language pack and security questionnaire guidance for sales/legal. Outcome: a repeatable packaging decision framework and a short-run pricing experiment to validate willingness-to-pay and sales friction.

title: Tiered Security Addendum & Pricing Experiment for SSO/SAML + RBAC
preview: Problem statement: Enterprise customers frequently require contractual commitments for access control (SSO/SAML) and role-based access control (RBAC). Currently these appear as ad-hoc addenda negotiated during sales cycles which increases legal time and sales friction. We need a product-led packaging experiment that...
text:
Problem statement: Enterprise customers frequently require contractual commitments for access control (SSO/SAML) and role-based access control (RBAC). Currently these appear as ad-hoc addenda negotiated during sales cycles which increases legal time and sales friction. We need a product-led packaging experiment that standardizes a small set of contractable security addenda tied to explicit pricing/commitment tiers so sales and legal can offer predictable options without lengthy negotiation.

Goals: 1) Define 2-3 standardized 'Security Addendum' tiers that can be attached to Hosted and Dedicated offerings: Basic (SSO/SAML support + standard RBAC), Enhanced (SLA-backed RBAC enforcement + audit log retention), and Isolated (private tenant + KMS integration + custom RBAC mappings). 2) Determine attachment pricing or commitment thresholds for each tier and run an experiment with a subset o...

title: Consolidate SIG/CAIQ responses and standardized customer attestations for pricing tiers
preview: Goal: Create a single authoritative SIG/CAIQ response bundle and a set of tiered customer attestation templates that map to Redwood pricing/packaging tiers. This reduces sales cycles, ensures consistent legal posture across deals, and enables automated artifact generation for self-serve and sales-assisted flows. Sco...
text: Goal: Create a single authoritative SIG/CAIQ response bundle and a set of tiered customer attestation templates that map to Redwood pricing/packaging tiers. This reduces sales cycles, ensures consistent legal posture across deals, and enables automated artifact generation for self-serve and sales-assisted flows. Scope: inventory existing responses, reconcile conflicts between engineering artifacts and legal guidance, produce a canonical CAIQ answer pack (v1) plus three attestation templates (Self-Serve, Dedicated, Private) with agreed scoping language. Deliverables: canonical CAIQ JSON+PDF, three attestation templates, a lightweight sales playbook page, a generator script to inject customer-specific values, and a legal sign-off checklist.

title: Enterprise dedicated request gating and prioritization framework
preview: Problem: We receive ad-hoc enterprise requests for Dedicated (reserved) capacity that vary widely in SLA expectations, throughput requirements, and hardware compatibility. Currently intake lacks consistent gating criteria, leading to mismatched capacity commits, last-minute engineering scope increases, and elevated ...
text:
Problem: We receive ad-hoc enterprise requests for Dedicated (reserved) capacity that vary widely in SLA expectations, throughput requirements, and hardware compatibility. Currently intake lacks consistent gating criteria, leading to mismatched capacity commits, last-minute engineering scope increases, and elevated operational risk.

Goal: Define a lightweight but rigorous intake and prioritization framework that product and solutions teams can use to triage enterprise Dedicated requests. The framework should: 1) capture minimal required signal to assess feasibility (SLA targets, steady vs burst throughput, GPU class preference, region/VPC constraints, data residency), 2) produce a prioritization score combining customer strategic value and operational risk, and 3) recommend concrete next steps (pilot, reserved commit, or decline) and engineering scope.

Scope: This is a non-bug produ...

title: Enterprise staging pin reservation & risk-buffer playbook for model catalog onboarding
preview: Problem: Enterprise customers frequently request model pins and new model additions that have high business impact but varying risk profiles (latency, PII exfiltration surface, quantization quality). Current intake is binary (approve/reject) which causes either expensive emergency rollbacks or lengthy hold times. Pr...
text:
Problem: Enterprise customers frequently request model pins and new model additions that have high business impact but varying risk profiles (latency, PII exfiltration surface, quantization quality). Current intake is binary (approve/reject) which causes either expensive emergency rollbacks or lengthy hold times. Proposal: introduce an 'Enterprise Staging Pin Reservation' playbook that creates a temporary, time-boxed pin with an attached risk-buffer and mandatory eval gates. This playbook standardizes decision criteria, operational controls, and provides a fast but safe path to grant enterprises short-term stability while the model completes full onboarding.

Key goals:
- Reduce mean time to pin approval for high-value enterprise requests from days to <48 hours for staged pins.
- Prevent long-lived, unreviewed pins by enforcing automatic expiry and deprecation triggers.
- Tie pin issu...

title: Geofence AuditStream: automated control-to-artifact mapping for data residency & retention evidence
preview:
Goal: build an automated 'AuditStream' that maps residency and retention controls to tamper-evident artifacts so SOC 2 / ISO 27001 auditors and customers can rapidly verify compliance for region-bound datasets.

Scope: produce evidence for three common control families: (1) Geofencing (data never leaves region X), (...
text:
Goal: build an automated 'AuditStream' that maps residency and retention controls to tamper-evident artifacts so SOC 2 / ISO 27001 auditors and customers can rapidly verify compliance for region-bound datasets.

Scope: produce evidence for three common control families: (1) Geofencing (data never leaves region X), (2) Retention policy enforcement (data deleted after N days, retention exceptions recorded), (3) Evidence collection & signing (artifact generation, hash anchoring, and access claims). This ticket covers the PM-level orchestration, control mapping matrix, acceptance criteria, and rollout plan. Implementation split into collectors (cloud telemetry, object metadata sweeps), an aggregator service that assembles artifacts, and a signer/manifest service that timestamps and stores proofs in a tamper-evident store.

Design notes: artifacts will be compact JSON manifests containing:...

title: Hybrid metering SKU and transient pricing phases for mixed token+compute plans
preview: PRD to define how Redwood bills hybrid plans that combine per-token usage with per-model compute (GPU-minutes) under a single customer SKU, including transient pricing phases when customers change plans mid-cycle and a customer-facing invoice preview experience.
text: PRD to define how Redwood bills hybrid plans that combine per-token usage with per-model compute (GPU-minutes) under a single customer SKU, including transient pricing phases when customers change plans mid-cycle and a customer-facing invoice preview experience.

title: Migration Accelerator: Beta Cohorts, In-Product Nudge Flows, and Release Notes Kit
preview: Goal: Deliver a reusable Migration Accelerator playbook and asset kit to make deprecations predictable, low-friction, and measurable for customers. This ticket covers three deliverables: 1) Beta cohort selection and operations checklist (segmentation, invite cadence, success criteria); 2) In-product nudge flows and ...
text:
Goal: Deliver a reusable Migration Accelerator playbook and asset kit to make deprecations predictable, low-friction, and measurable for customers. This ticket covers three deliverables: 1) Beta cohort selection and operations checklist (segmentation, invite cadence, success criteria); 2) In-product nudge flows and SDK warnings (banner copy, throttled reminders, SDK deprecation warnings, telemetry hooks); 3) Release notes kit and GTM cadence (email templates, blog + changelog entries, support-runbook, PRD for console surfaced warnings).

Scope: We are planning a staged sunset of api/v1-models to api/v2-models for a subset of legacy model variants. This is not a full platform-wide retirement. Workstreams include: cohort design (which customers to enroll for early migration assistance), in-product UX (console banners, modal flows), SDK/CLI update strategy (deprecation headers and opt-in...

title: Competitive compass: translating model-landscape signals into procurement & fallback decisions
preview: Problem: As the open-model ecosystem proliferates, our commercial teams and platform ops need a repeatable way to map external signals about model availability, pricing, latency, and feature drift into concrete procurement actions (reserve vs hosted), pricing guidance, and automated fallback rules. Scope: produce a ...
text: Problem: As the open-model ecosystem proliferates, our commercial teams and platform ops need a repeatable way to map external signals about model availability, pricing, latency, and feature drift into concrete procurement actions (reserve vs hosted), pricing guidance, and automated fallback rules. Scope: produce a signal-to-decision matrix, an operational rubric that weights signals (e.g., availability volatility, quantization quality, regional latency delta, contractual SLA gaps), and a one-page procurement decision playbook that can be used by PMs, sales-engineering, and Platform SREs. Deliverables: 1) prioritized signal taxonomy with weights and examples, 2) decision matrix mapping signal bands to procurement actions and fallback policies, 3) playbook doc + template RFP checklist for enterprise deals, 4) instrumentation spec for ops (what to surface in Console and Alerts). This ti...

title: Demand heatmap + feasibility snapshot for Sales/CS enterprise asks
preview: Create a compact, repeatable intake artifact that Sales/CS can use to consolidate enterprise feature requests into a demand heatmap and a one-page feasibility snapshot. The goal is to reduce lead time from first-sales-ask to an actionable PM/Eng scoping decision from weeks to days. This ticket delivers: 1) an intake...
text: Create a compact, repeatable intake artifact that Sales/CS can use to consolidate enterprise feature requests into a demand heatmap and a one-page feasibility snapshot. The goal is to reduce lead time from first-sales-ask to an actionable PM/Eng scoping decision from weeks to days. This ticket delivers: 1) an intake template for frontline reps that balances signal capture with low friction, 2) an automated scoring rubric (ARR, #accounts, strategic fit, legal/regulatory risk, infra/runtime compatibility), 3) a template "feasibility snapshot" that includes rough effort band, risks, suggested model/runtime path (hosted / dedicated / private), and 4) a simple dashboard CSV schema for aggregation and heatmap generation.

title: Pinning playbook: operator UX and policies for model-version pin + graceful fallback
preview: Problem: As teams adopt model version pinning and staged rollouts (canary / A-B), interactions across region selection, capacity tiers (hosted/dedicated/private), and model variant fallbacks create subtle production behaviors that increase error rates, latency variability, and surprise cost exposure. This ticket pro...
text:
Problem: As teams adopt model version pinning and staged rollouts (canary / A-B), interactions across region selection, capacity tiers (hosted/dedicated/private), and model variant fallbacks create subtle production behaviors that increase error rates, latency variability, and surprise cost exposure. This ticket proposes a product playbook and operator UX requirements to make pinning + rollout behavior explicit, safe, and observable for customers.

Goals:
- Define deterministic policy semantics that compose: region preference, tier ordering (dedicated -> hosted -> private fallback), version pin TTL and cohort rules, and model-variant degrade paths.
- Surface these policies in Console and API with clear defaults and override knobs for advanced users.
- Provide monitoring, alerts, and bounded cost/latency SLAs during rollouts.
- Ship a customer-facing runbook "Pin + Rollout Safety Playb...

title: Observability-driven acceptance for streamed function-call flows and failure classification
preview: Define clear, testable acceptance criteria for structured output and function/tool-calling behavior in streaming responses, with an emphasis on observable failure classification and graceful degradation. This ticket captures requirements that the runtime, gateway, and SDKs must satisfy before we mark the feature GA....
text: Define clear, testable acceptance criteria for structured output and function/tool-calling behavior in streaming responses, with an emphasis on observable failure classification and graceful degradation. This ticket captures requirements that the runtime, gateway, and SDKs must satisfy before we mark the feature GA. The goal is to protect downstream consumers (apps and analytics) from silently corrupted or partial tool invocations and to provide deterministic signals for operators to triage issues quickly.

title: Reservation authorization and capacity workbench for Dedicated pools
preview: Goal: Define and implement a bounded, auditable reservation request and approval workflow plus an operator workbench that ties throughput guarantees, autoscaling policy templates, and fleet controls into the Dedicated provisioning lifecycle. This ticket scopes the PM deliverables: request form schema, approval matri...
text:
Goal: Define and implement a bounded, auditable reservation request and approval workflow plus an operator workbench that ties throughput guarantees, autoscaling policy templates, and fleet controls into the Dedicated provisioning lifecycle. This ticket scopes the PM deliverables: request form schema, approval matrix, capacity planning heuristics, UX sketches for the workbench, and the verification playbook for provisioning events.

Context: Several early customers are adopting Dedicated and ask for a single place to request guaranteed throughput (tokens/sec or requests/sec), choose autoscaling behaviors (aggressive, balanced, conservative), and indicate fleet constraints (allowed regions, GPU families, maintenance windows). Engineers want predictable capacity sizing and test hooks to validate SLOs after an allocation. Current ad-hoc requests cause back-and-forth between sales, SRE, a...

title: Bucketed commit tier simulator with telemetry gating probe
preview: Run a simulation-backed probe of a bucketed commit tier model that (1) groups reserved credit commitments into discrete buckets, (2) credits short-term streaming bursts via an on-ramp grace mechanism, and (3) applies telemetry gating to reduce gaming/abuse. This ticket scopes the simulation, acceptance criteria, req...
text: Run a simulation-backed probe of a bucketed commit tier model that (1) groups reserved credit commitments into discrete buckets, (2) credits short-term streaming bursts via an on-ramp grace mechanism, and (3) applies telemetry gating to reduce gaming/abuse. This ticket scopes the simulation, acceptance criteria, required artifacts for sales/legal preflight, and pilot plan for 6 pilot customers.

title: Console policy composer — region priorities, tier routing, and staged model fallbacks
preview: Build a customer-facing Policy Composer in the Redwood Console that lets customers express routing preferences across regions, capacity tiers (hot/warm/cold), and ordered fallback model variants. The composer should be approachable for PM/Dev users (visual rule blocks + guided defaults) while remaining expressive fo...
text: Build a customer-facing Policy Composer in the Redwood Console that lets customers express routing preferences across regions, capacity tiers (hot/warm/cold), and ordered fallback model variants. The composer should be approachable for PM/Dev users (visual rule blocks + guided defaults) while remaining expressive for advanced users (JSON/DLS export/import). The feature's goals are: 1) enable customers to implement region-first rules with soft or hard preferences, 2) let customers specify tier routing policies with autoscaling/burst hints, and 3) author staged fallback chains of model variants (eg. prefer quantized cheaper model, fallback to full-precision model on quality or capacity failure). Include real-time validation, conflict warnings, and a preview simulator that runs a dry-run against synthetic traces to estimate latency/cost hit and failure modes.

title: Granular lane cost & SLA cutover PRD + deployment checklist for hosted inference endpoints
preview: Summary: This PRD defines the product requirements, acceptance criteria, and launch checklist for introducing granular lane pricing and SLA profiles across the hosted inference endpoints (chat/completions/embeddings/rerank). The goal is to expose cost-tiered lanes that let customers trade off latency and price while...
text: Summary: This PRD defines the product requirements, acceptance criteria, and launch checklist for introducing granular lane pricing and SLA profiles across the hosted inference endpoints (chat/completions/embeddings/rerank). The goal is to expose cost-tiered lanes that let customers trade off latency and price while allowing Redwood to perform zero-downtime cutovers and graded degradations between lanes when capacity or incidents occur. The feature surface includes: lane definition and metadata, per-lane SLA and cost band configuration, API surface to request or opt into lanes, backend routing rules and fallback policy, telemetry and billing hooks, canary and cutover playbook, and migration guidance for existing customers.

title: Enterprise data controls intake workflow and priority decomposition
preview: Create a clear, implementable product-side intake and prioritization workflow for enterprise data-control requests (logging redaction, retention policy alterations, PII handling, and audit export formats). This ticket captures the decision criteria, priority decomposition, routing rules to GTM/SE/Eng, and stubbed re...
text: Create a clear, implementable product-side intake and prioritization workflow for enterprise data-control requests (logging redaction, retention policy alterations, PII handling, and audit export formats). This ticket captures the decision criteria, priority decomposition, routing rules to GTM/SE/Eng, and stubbed responses for the Console and Support intake UI. Aim: reduce time-to-response for security-sensitive requests from days to <48 hours for initial triage and map each request to an owner and SLA band.

title: Routing Governor: interactive Console sandbox for region/tier selection and fallback variants
preview: Build an interactive 'Routing Governor' sandbox in the Redwood Console that lets customers author, validate, and preview routing policies that express region selection, tier routing, and tiered fallback model variants. This feature surfaces validation hints, live cost/latency estimates, and a simulated traffic previ...
text: Build an interactive 'Routing Governor' sandbox in the Redwood Console that lets customers author, validate, and preview routing policies that express region selection, tier routing, and tiered fallback model variants. This feature surfaces validation hints, live cost/latency estimates, and a simulated traffic preview so product/platform teams can safely iterate on policies before applying them.

title: Dedicated: buffer telemetry, grace periods, and preemption visibility PRD
preview: This PRD defines a set of telemetry, console UX, and API changes to make tenant "buffer" consumption and grace-period behavior observable and controllable for Dedicated customers. Teams need clear signals when a tenant is borrowing burst credits, when grace periods are active, and why preemptions/evictions occurred....
text: This PRD defines a set of telemetry, console UX, and API changes to make tenant "buffer" consumption and grace-period behavior observable and controllable for Dedicated customers. Teams need clear signals when a tenant is borrowing burst credits, when grace periods are active, and why preemptions/evictions occurred. Current gaps cause customer confusion and increase SRE toil during capacity incidents.

title: On-demand Attestation Marketplace + Usage-triggered Crediting Pilot
preview: Problem: Enterprise deals frequently stall on customized security questionnaires (SIG/CAIQ). Current attestation support is manual and creates uneven sales lift and high engineering/solutions effort. Goal: pilot an on-demand attestation marketplace plus a usage-triggered crediting model so customers can self-request...
text: Problem: Enterprise deals frequently stall on customized security questionnaires (SIG/CAIQ). Current attestation support is manual and creates uneven sales lift and high engineering/solutions effort. Goal: pilot an on-demand attestation marketplace plus a usage-triggered crediting model so customers can self-request curated, signed attestation bundles while sales gets credits to accelerate legal/security review. The pilot should reduce days-to-contract, lower SE hours per deal, and surface repeatable answer packs for automation.

title: Pilot: Enterprise Contract Tiering for SSO/SAML & RBAC Requirements
preview: Background: Large enterprise customers repeatedly list SSO/SAML and fine-grained RBAC in security questionnaires as gating items for contract signature. Sales teams are pushing for a clear product+contract offering that defines minimum commitments (SLA, support, on-boarding) tied to accessible packaging and predicta...
text:
Background: Large enterprise customers repeatedly list SSO/SAML and fine-grained RBAC in security questionnaires as gating items for contract signature. Sales teams are pushing for a clear product+contract offering that defines minimum commitments (SLA, support, on-boarding) tied to accessible packaging and predictable pricing. This ticket scopes a small pricing/packaging pilot that ties explicit SSO/SAML and RBAC deliverables to contract tiers, and verifies downstream impacts (legal reviews, runtime access patterns, engineering delivery).

Goal: Define a minimal, testable contract tier matrix (three tiers) that includes SSO/SAML configuration commitments, RBAC feature coverage, and corresponding commercial terms (price uplift, committed usage, delivery windows). Run a pilot with 2–3 inbound enterprise prospects to validate willingness-to-pay and operational feasibility. Produce a sin...

title: Pricing commitment matrix & security questionnaire alignment for SSO/RBAC contract variability
preview: Run a pricing/packaging experiment and create canonical security-questionnaire answers tying SSO (SAML/SCIM) and RBAC commitments to contractual packaging. Goal: reduce negotiation friction, standardize commitments across tiers, and measure lift in close rate and deal size when we offer clearly-scoped SSO/RBAC commi...
text: Run a pricing/packaging experiment and create canonical security-questionnaire answers tying SSO (SAML/SCIM) and RBAC commitments to contractual packaging. Goal: reduce negotiation friction, standardize commitments across tiers, and measure lift in close rate and deal size when we offer clearly-scoped SSO/RBAC commitments vs bespoke offers.

title: Tranche Playbook for Enterprise-Isolated Deploys: Quarterly Cadence, Risk Budget, and Acceptance Gates
preview: Summary: Create a repeatable, tranche-based quarterly playbook for delivering Private / VPC / on-prem features to enterprise customers. The playbook will define tranche boundaries (canary -> wave -> general availability), a risk budget model (time, rollback windows, compatibility debt), acceptance gates, cross-funct...
text:
Summary: Create a repeatable, tranche-based quarterly playbook for delivering Private / VPC / on-prem features to enterprise customers. The playbook will define tranche boundaries (canary -> wave -> general availability), a risk budget model (time, rollback windows, compatibility debt), acceptance gates, cross-functional roles/responsibilities, and observability/signals tied to rollout decisions.

Scope:
- Operational playbook for sequencing quarterly deployments across gated tranches (Tier 1 canaries, Tier 2 waves, Tier 3 GA).
- Standardized acceptance criteria and telemetry thresholds for promoting between tranches.
- Rollback and fallback policies mapped to model/runtime versions, upgrade windows, and customer cohorts.
- Communication templates, runbook checklist, and stakeholder cadence (PM/Eng/Support/Customer Success).
- Risk budget spreadsheet and escalation paths for SLA degra...

title: Delegated sign-off matrix and cryptographic audit proofs for staged model rollouts
preview: Goal: Define and deliver a flexible approval and change-management system for canary/A-B/shadow rollouts that combines RBAC-driven delegation, time-bound sign-offs, and tamper-evident approval records. The system should support: (1) configurable approver matrices per environment (dev/stage/prod), (2) delegated appro...
text: Goal: Define and deliver a flexible approval and change-management system for canary/A-B/shadow rollouts that combines RBAC-driven delegation, time-bound sign-offs, and tamper-evident approval records. The system should support: (1) configurable approver matrices per environment (dev/stage/prod), (2) delegated approvers with expiry windows and secondary approver requirements, (3) cryptographic signing of approvals (short-lived JWTs signed by KMS) to attach to rollout decisions, (4) exportable, queryable audit logs for compliance and postmortems, and (5) integration points for auto-rollback triggers tied to SLOs and quality regressions.

title: Market Thread Mapping for Model-Substitute Cascades
preview: Objective: produce a granular "market-thread" map that predicts how a change in upstream model availability or pricing (e.g., an outage, embargo, or sudden price delta) cascades across customer segments, regions, and feature surfaces. The map should surface operational triggers for procurement, recommended fallback ...
text: Objective: produce a granular "market-thread" map that predicts how a change in upstream model availability or pricing (e.g., an outage, embargo, or sudden price delta) cascades across customer segments, regions, and feature surfaces. The map should surface operational triggers for procurement, recommended fallback model variants, and comms playbooks for Sales/CS.

title: Interim usage quarantine and automated tenant-credit simulator for contested enterprise invoices
preview: Summary: Enterprise customers sometimes raise security questionnaire or contract questions that result in a temporary hold/escrow on invoices while usage is investigated. Current operations require manual ledger edits and provisional credits created by Finance, which is slow and error-prone for high-volume metered a...
text: Summary: Enterprise customers sometimes raise security questionnaire or contract questions that result in a temporary hold/escrow on invoices while usage is investigated. Current operations require manual ledger edits and provisional credits created by Finance, which is slow and error-prone for high-volume metered accounts. This ticket scopes a short-lived product/ops capability: (1) a usage quarantine flow that isolates suspect usage from final invoicing, and (2) an automated tenant-credit simulator (sandbox) that lets CS/Finance/PMs preview prorations, provisional credits, and final invoice outcomes before accepting/committing ledger changes. Goal: reduce mean time-to-resolution for contested usage, lower manual adjustment errors, and provide reproducible experiments for pricing/packaging A/Bs that touch credits/proration logic.
